paper.title: Environmental durability of externally bonded FRP materials intended for repair of concrete structures
paper.authors: J.R. Cromwell; K.A. Harries; B.M. Shahrooz
paper.notes: Concrete strength is reported only as a specimen-context average for FRP bond/flexure tests; mixture composition is not provided.

strength_results.notes: Average for bond-test cubes; mixture composition missing.

evidence.evidence_text: Environmental durability of externally bonded FRP materials intended for repair of concrete structures J.R. Cromwell a, K.A. Harries b,⇑, B.M. Shahrooz c a THP Limited, Cincinnati, United States b Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering, University of Pittsburgh, United States c School of Advanced Structures, University of Cincinnati, United States article info Article history: Received 22 June 2010 Received in revised form 17 November 2010 Accepted 17 November 2010 Available online 18
The average 56-day compressive strength for all 192 cubes was 30.3 MPa. The concrete compressive strength determined at the age of testing (approximately 147 days) was 38 MPa.

review_queue.question: Decide whether contextual concrete cube strength without mixture proportions belongs in the database.
review_queue.suggested_action: Use only as reviewed contextual strength unless the source PDF provides a matching mix design.

main_risks: Concrete mixture composition is not supplied in the packet.
